response.justification: Bogdan has broad full-stack experience and genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, which is directionally relevant, but the core technical requirements of this role — modern C++ or Rust development — are essentially absent from his recent career. His C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project; there is no Rust anywhere on the resume. His current and recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js. The geospatial work he has done is at the web/application layer rather than in low-level data pipeline or routing tile processing contexts, and there is no evidence of experience with routing guidance systems, tile generation, or traffic data integration.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective open-source platform and Green Map GIS work, demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts and FOSS4G community
High-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic, handling millions of requests per second with low-latency async I/O in DLang — shows comfort with performance-sensitive infrastructure
Strong engineering discipline: consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices across roles, suggesting adaptable and quality-focused developer
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014–2015, ~6 months) and limited in scope; no Rust experience at all — both are primary technical requirements for this role
Recent 5+ years of work is exclusively TypeScript/Node.js; there is no demonstrated path back to systems-level programming in the immediate employment history
Geospatial work is web/application-layer (Ember.js, Node.js GIS platform) with no exposure to routing tile pipelines, navigation data processing, or real-time traffic signal integration
